55dca3cb-7eca-4e63-9c51-184be5a9617f.name: Ava
55dca3cb-7eca-4e63-9c51-184be5a9617f.content:
Strategic Direction — helixir Platform

PRD written 2026-03-04. See PRD file at .automaker/projects/helixir-platform/prd.md

Vision

Transform helixir from an MCP server into THE web component developer platform. Core analysis engine + extensions (CLI, GitHub Action, VS Code, wrapper generator, docs, Storybook, dashboard).

Priority

Phase 1: Programmatic API export (unlock everything). Phase 2: CLI subcommands (10x addressable market). Phase 3: GitHub Action (enterprise viral loop). Phase 4+: VS Code, wrapper gen, docs, Storybook, dashboard.

Architecture

Monorepo with packages/. Core stays as helixir npm package. Extensions are separate packages sharing types. pnpm workspaces.

Decisions — 2026-03-04

  • Brand name: helixkit (npm, VS Code extension "HelixKit", brew install helixkit)
  • GitHub org: bookedsolidtech
  • Version strategy: stay pre-release, next minor is 0.2.0
  • No JetBrains plugin (dropped from scope)
  • Rename later: repo + directory rename from helixir to helixkit deferred. All new work assumes helixkit branding.

Check-in — 2026-03-18 04:10 UTC

ENTIRE EPIC BUILT. F1 merged. F2 (PR #86), F3 (PR #87), F4 (PR #88) all in review. Systemic test failures across all 3 — agents fixing. Helix team active on a11y fixes in their repo. Done count: 9. Board: 15 features total.

Status — 2026-03-18 05:10 UTC

Full epic completed. All 4 health dimension features (F1-F4) merged to dev. Board: 13 done, 2 blocked (Jake decisions), 0 backlog. PR #89 (dev→staging) opened. 8 PRs shipping tonight: 4 bug fixes + 4 epic features. Helix team notified of hx-stat.ts merge conflict (issue #976). PR #83 still needs helix team. Next: monitor PR #89 CI, then staging→main.

Status — 2026-03-18 07:10 UTC

PR #89 (dev→staging) merged at 06:09 UTC. PR #90 (staging→main) auto-created, 17/18 checks passing. HITL gate — awaiting Jake's approval to release to main. This batch includes new features beyond bug fixes, so respecting the gate. Helix team still quiet (no responses to #84 or #976).

Status — 2026-03-18 10:15 UTC

RELEASED TO MAIN. PR #90 merged (4e74a96). Full overnight epic + bug fixes now on main. Awaiting changeset-release bot for version bump. The "Changeset Required" check was the stuck pending check on all promotion PRs — filing as system improvement candidate.

Status — 2026-03-19 17:05 UTC

Board: 18 features — 14 done, 2 review (stale), 2 blocked. Both review features (PR #93, PR #97) are already merged to main but stuck in review — filed bug on protoMaker board (merged-PR reconciliation not catching PRs targeting main instead of dev). 2 blocked features still awaiting Jake decisions: CLA bot (CLA vs DCO) and package manager standardization (npm vs pnpm). Auto-mode off, 0 backlog. No running agents. No promotion needed (staging delta = 0).

Status — 2026-03-19 18:30 UTC

pnpm standardization: PR #98 created, 8/9 functional checks passed. Audit check failed (turbo transitive dep vulnerability). Auto-merge enabled — Jake removing audit from required checks. CodeRabbit thread resolved (turbo recursion architecture decision).

Expert health scoring audits launched: 3 features created, 2 started in parallel:

  • A11y dimension enhancement audit (accessibility-engineer, Opus) — running
  • Lit best practices scoring proposal (lit-specialist, Opus) — running
  • Audit doc alignment (depends on both above) — backlog, blocked on deps

Decision recorded: pnpm is the org-wide standard (Jake confirmed).

Status — 2026-03-21 03:00 UTC (Hour 1)

8-hour self-improvement sprint: Shadow DOM styling diagnostics.

  • Created comprehensive shadow-dom-styling-guide.md context file (injected into ALL agent prompts) — covers anti-patterns, correct approaches, library-specific dark mode implementations, framework gotchas
  • Created 5 new features on board: diagnose_styling tool (P1), check_shadow_dom_usage anti-pattern detector (P1), suggest_usage styling enhancement (P1), CSS Architecture scoring sub-dimensions (P2), detect_theme_support dark mode checker (P2)
  • Dependencies set: P2 features depend on P1 diagnose_styling
  • Auto-mode started (2 concurrent) — agents picking up P1 backlog
  • Deep local research across 16 wc-libraries: catalogued :host, ::part, ::slotted, prefers-color-scheme, color-scheme patterns from Shoelace, Material Web, Spectrum, Lion, Elix, Ionic, Vaadin, FAST, Calcite
  • Key finding: suggest_usage and narrative handlers have ZERO styling guidance — primary hallucination source identified

Status — 2026-03-21 03:17 UTC (11:17pm ET)

Shipped: PR #103 — Shadow DOM styling diagnostic MCP tools (diagnose_styling + check_shadow_dom_usage). 48 new tests, build clean. Implemented locally after agent worktrees lost code from escalation cycle.

In Review: PRs #101 (suggest_usage styling), #102 (CSS Architecture scoring), #103 (styling tools), #104 (detect_theme_support). CI fixing in progress.

Context Files Created: shadow-dom-styling-guide.md, styling-cookbook.md, component-composition-guide.md — all injected into agent prompts.

Agents: 1 escalation handler on PR #101. 2 helix audit agents (error handling, i18n). Researching framework integration patterns for next context file.

Next: Fix CI on PR #103, create framework-integration-patterns context file, review and merge passing PRs, continue wc-libraries research for more tool ideas.

Status — 2026-03-25 03:55 UTC

Enterprise Readiness project created. 8 features + 2 epics on the board, fully sequenced with dependencies.

  • Board cleanup: 3 stale escalation features (PR #98/#101/#102 escalations) marked done. PR #104 (detect_theme_support) kept — still has failing CI, now tracked as enterprise-readiness feature.
  • Dependency graph: @helixui/mcp core (foundation, P1) + fix PR #104 (P1, no deps) can start immediately in parallel. scaffold_component and extend_component depend on core. create_theme depends on core + PR #104 fix. Docs depend on tools being built. CI action depends on core.
  • CLA vs DCO: Recommended DCO to Jake. Awaiting confirmation before unblocking the CLA bot feature.
  • Enterprise engagement: Mid-April 2026 deadline. Team has mixed Lit/WC experience — balanced scaffolding tools + documentation approach confirmed by Jake.
